# Extractor Receipt

## Summary

This source contains empirical evidence about open-source adoption of AI configuration files, the kinds of information and writing styles found in AGENTS.md files, and how AGENTS.md files change over time.

## Evidence Files

### file: evidence-01.md
```markdown
---
version: evidence.v1
article_id: art-2026-03-04-003
evidence_id: evidence-01
kind: metric
citation: Mohsenimofidi et al., 2026, §4.1
source_url: https://arxiv.org/html/2510.21413
confidence: high
mechanism_relevance: none
contradiction_relevance: none
---
Only 466 of the scanned repositories, or 5%, had adopted at least one of the AI configuration file formats the study considered.

## Source Excerpt

"Only 466 (5%) of the repositories that we scanned had already adopted at least one of the formats that we considered."

## Note

The reported adoption rate is for the file formats included in this study.
```

### file: evidence-02.md
```markdown
---
version: evidence.v1
article_id: art-2026-03-04-003
evidence_id: evidence-02
kind: claim-support
citation: Mohsenimofidi et al., 2026, §4.1
source_url: https://arxiv.org/html/2510.21413
confidence: medium
mechanism_relevance: bounded
contradiction_relevance: tension
---
The adoption analysis covered four commercial tools and did not include other tools such as Cline or OpenCode.

## Source Excerpt

"However, we focused only on four commercial tools, although other commercial or open-source alternatives such as Cline and OpenCode exist."

## Note

This bounds the adoption result because the measured 5% rate excludes other AI configuration ecosystems the paper names as possible future additions.
```

### file: evidence-03.md
```markdown
---
version: evidence.v1
article_id: art-2026-03-04-003
evidence_id: evidence-03
kind: metric
citation: Mohsenimofidi et al., 2026, §4.1
source_url: https://arxiv.org/html/2510.21413
confidence: high
mechanism_relevance: none
contradiction_relevance: none
---
Among repositories with AI configuration files, the language counts reported were 135 TypeScript, 58 Go, 58 Python, 56 C#, 36 Java, 34 JavaScript, 32 C++, 29 Rust, 19 PHP, and 9 C.

## Source Excerpt

"We found AI configuration files in 135 repositories with TypeScript as main language, 58 Go, 58 Python, 56 C#, 36 Java, 34 JavaScript, 32 C++, 29 Rust, 19 PHP, and 9 C."

## Note

These are counts for repositories with AI configuration files, not overall language prevalence on GitHub.
```

### file: evidence-04.md
```markdown
---
version: evidence.v1
article_id: art-2026-03-04-003
evidence_id: evidence-04
kind: metric
citation: Mohsenimofidi et al., 2026, §4.1
source_url: https://arxiv.org/html/2510.21413
confidence: high
mechanism_relevance: none
contradiction_relevance: none
---
Copilot instruction files were the longest on average at 310 lines, while AGENTS.md files showed the highest length variation with mean 142 lines and standard deviation 231.

## Source Excerpt

"The Copilot instruction files were on average the longest ( M = 310 lines, SD = 127 lines)... AGENTS.md files had the highest variation ( M = 142, SD = 231 )."

## Note

The source reports file length in lines and explicitly distinguishes average length from variation.
```

### file: evidence-05.md
```markdown
---
version: evidence.v1
article_id: art-2026-03-04-003
evidence_id: evidence-05
kind: method
citation: Mohsenimofidi et al., 2026, §4.2
source_url: https://arxiv.org/html/2510.21413
confidence: high
mechanism_relevance: none
contradiction_relevance: tension
---
For the AGENTS.md heading analysis, the authors excluded 15 files created before January 1, 2025 and excluded 5 files with no heading structure.

## Source Excerpt

"We excluded 15 AGENTS.md files that were created before January 1, 2025, i.e., before the AGENTS.md convention was introduced."
"Five repositories contained AGENTS.md files without any heading structure; these were excluded from the analysis."

## Note

These exclusions limit the structural analysis to post-convention files that also use headings.
```

### file: evidence-06.md
```markdown
---
version: evidence.v1
article_id: art-2026-03-04-003
evidence_id: evidence-06
kind: metric
citation: Mohsenimofidi et al., 2026, Table 1
source_url: https://arxiv.org/html/2510.21413
confidence: high
mechanism_relevance: none
contradiction_relevance: none
---
The three most common level-1 or level-2 heading categories in AGENTS.md were conventions/best practices (50), contribution guidelines (48), and architecture/project structure (47).

## Source Excerpt

"Conventions/best practices ... 50"
"Contribution guidelines ... 48"
"Architecture and project structure ... 47"

## Note

The counts are heading-category frequencies at levels 1 or 2, as reported in Table 1.
```

### file: evidence-07.md
```markdown
---
version: evidence.v1
article_id: art-2026-03-04-003
evidence_id: evidence-07
kind: result
citation: Mohsenimofidi et al., 2026, §4.2
source_url: https://arxiv.org/html/2510.21413
confidence: high
mechanism_relevance: none
contradiction_relevance: none
---
The authors characterized AGENTS.md writing style along five dimensions: descriptive, prescriptive, prohibitive, explanatory, and conditional.

## Source Excerpt

"We found that the writing style can be characterized along five stylistic dimensions: descriptive, prescriptive, prohibitive, explanatory, and conditional."

## Note

The source presents these as observed stylistic dimensions in the analyzed sections.
```

### file: evidence-08.md
```markdown
---
version: evidence.v1
article_id: art-2026-03-04-003
evidence_id: evidence-08
kind: quote
citation: Mohsenimofidi et al., 2026, §4.2
source_url: https://arxiv.org/html/2510.21413
confidence: high
mechanism_relevance: none
contradiction_relevance: none
---
The source gives "Never commit directly to the main branch" as an example of a prohibitive instruction in AGENTS.md.

## Source Excerpt

"Prohibitive statements were also common, explicitly indicating what not to do, e.g., 'Never commit directly to the main branch.'"

## Note

This is presented as an example of prohibitive style, not as a frequency estimate.
```

### file: evidence-09.md
```markdown
---
version: evidence.v1
article_id: art-2026-03-04-003
evidence_id: evidence-09
kind: metric
citation: Mohsenimofidi et al., 2026, §4.3
source_url: https://arxiv.org/html/2510.21413
confidence: high
mechanism_relevance: none
contradiction_relevance: none
---
Among 155 AGENTS.md files, 77 had no changes after the initial commit, 36 changed once, and 32 changed between two and seven times.

## Source Excerpt

"77 (50%) of them had not been changed after the initial commit, 36 (23%) only once, and 32 (21%) between two and seven times."

## Note

These counts describe observed post-creation change frequency in the sampled AGENTS.md histories.
```

### file: evidence-10.md
```markdown
---
version: evidence.v1
article_id: art-2026-03-04-003
evidence_id: evidence-10
kind: method
citation: Mohsenimofidi et al., 2026, §4.3
source_url: https://arxiv.org/html/2510.21413
confidence: high
mechanism_relevance: none
contradiction_relevance: tension
---
The detailed evolution analysis focused on 10 files with at least 10 commits, producing a sample of 169 commits, or 37% of the 453 collected commits.

## Source Excerpt

"For this study, we decided to focus on 10 files (6%) with at least 10 commits, which yielded a sample of 169 commits to annotate (37% of the 453 collected commits)."

## Note

This narrows the commit-category analysis to the most frequently changed files rather than all AGENTS.md histories.
```

### file: evidence-11.md
```markdown
---
version: evidence.v1
article_id: art-2026-03-04-003
evidence_id: evidence-11
kind: metric
citation: Mohsenimofidi et al., 2026, §4.3 and Table 2
source_url: https://arxiv.org/html/2510.21413
confidence: high
mechanism_relevance: none
contradiction_relevance: none
---
Most annotated commits, 111 out of 169 or 66%, represented only one change category.

## Source Excerpt

"most commits (111, 66%) represented only one change category."

## Note

The unit of analysis here is commit-level category assignment in the annotated sample.
```

### file: evidence-12.md
```markdown
---
version: evidence.v1
article_id: art-2026-03-04-003
evidence_id: evidence-12
kind: metric
citation: Mohsenimofidi et al., 2026, Table 2
source_url: https://arxiv.org/html/2510.21413
confidence: high
mechanism_relevance: none
contradiction_relevance: none
---
The most frequent AGENTS.md change categories were add instruction(s) with 78 instances and modify instruction(s) with 59 instances.

## Source Excerpt

"Table 2 shows that the most frequent change categories are 'Add instruction(s)' and 'Modify instruction(s)'."
"Add instruction(s) ... 78"
"Modify instruction(s) ... 59"

## Note

These frequencies are category counts across the analyzed files in Table 2.
---

# Extractor Receipt

## Summary

This source contains empirical evidence about open-source adoption of AI configuration files, the kinds of information and writing styles found in AGENTS.md files, and how AGENTS.md files change over time.

## Evidence Files

### file: evidence-01.md
```markdown
---
version: evidence.v1
article_id: art-2026-03-04-003

source_url: https://arxiv.org/html/2510.21413
confidence: high
mechanism_relevance: none
contradiction_relevance: none
---
Only 466 of the scanned repositories, or 5%, had adopted at least one of the AI configuration file formats the study considered.

## Source Excerpt

"Only 466 (5%) of the repositories that we scanned had already adopted at least one of the formats that we considered."

## Note

The reported adoption rate is for the file formats included in this study.
```
